paper_id: Aguiar et al. - 2018 - Ontological Representation of Relational Databases

models.model_label: RDBS-O (Relational Database System Ontology) and its operational version RDBS-OWL

evidence_quotes.quote: In addition to a reference ontology, we also implement and test its operational version, using it to automatic instantiate the ontology from a real database sample.
The RDBS-O ontology, presented in Section 3, was implemented in OWL, giving rise to its operational version RDBS-OWL.
Furthermore, the RDBS-OWL operational version was applied in a semi-automatic approach that instantiates its concepts from a database.
An ontology-based solution would allow us to add support to any RDBMS by mapping its particular concepts to those of the ontology.

goals.justification: Per the Intervening definition and signal pattern 'implemented', the authors actually implemented the ontology in OWL (RDBS-OWL) and applied it in a semi-automatic approach that instantiates concepts from a real database. The model directly enables/guides creation of an operational artifact, satisfying Intervening.

evidence_quotes.quote: The Relational Database System Ontology (RDBS-O) aims to identify and represent key concepts of the relational database domain in the architectural scope, not covering control and execution details.
evidence_quotes.section: Section 3. The Relational Database System Ontology (RDBS-O)

evidence_quotes.quote: Therefore, concepts and relationships were identified and defined in a dictionary of terms [Aguiar 2018] to define and ensure consensual understanding of the domain.
evidence_quotes.section: Section 3. The Relational Database System Ontology (RDBS-O)

evidence_quotes.quote: The ontology incorporates concepts based on software engineering and database system ontologies in order to clarify the relationship with the relational database domain.

goals.justification: Per the Understanding definition and signal patterns ('clarify', 'define', 'shared understanding'), the authors explicitly frame the ontology as identifying/representing/clarifying key concepts and ensuring consensual understanding of the relational database domain.

evidence_quotes.quote: Therefore, concepts and relationships were identified and defined in a dictionary of terms [Aguiar 2018] to define and ensure consensual understanding of the domain.
evidence_quotes.section: Section 3. The Relational Database System Ontology (RDBS-O)

uncertain_goals.reason: The dictionary of terms suggests reference-like use, but the paper does not explicitly frame the ontology itself as a documentation/knowledge base artifact for asynchronous use by absent parties, so evidence is indirect.
